risk_rationale: First subtask in a brand-new package (engine/btree only has a doc.go placeholder). This subtask establishes the on-disk node byte layout (NodeSize, header fields, key/value encoding scheme) that every later btree subtask (1.2.2 lookup, 1.2.3 insert/split, 1.2.4 delete, 1.2.5 prefix scan, 1.2.6 persisted reload) will build directly on top of. A wrong or awkward format choice here would force a breaking rework in a later subtask.

downstream_consumers.dependency: consumes InternalNode/LeafNode struct shapes + Encode/Decode to traverse the tree
consumes node capacity model (NodeSize, error-on-overflow) to decide when to split
consumes LeafNode.NextLeaf sibling pointer added now specifically for this future use
consumes OpenIndexFile create-on-first-use helper and the on-disk node byte format

backward_compatibility: N/A - new package, no existing on-disk data or callers.
concurrency_considerations: None required by this subtask. Node encode/decode is a pure, stateless data transform with no shared mutable state. Latch-crabbing/optimistic-read concurrency (per docs/LLD/btree.md) is deferred to later subtasks that implement tree traversal/mutation.
rollback_plan: Single isolated commit adding two new files in a package with no existing consumers; revertable with a single git revert with no ripple effects.
